accounts package¶

Submodules¶

accounts.admin module¶

class accounts.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Admin configuration for CustomUser, extending the built-in UserAdmin.

fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role & Profile', {'fields': ('role', 'bio')}), ('Subscriptions', {'fields': ('subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff')¶
list_filter = ('role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')¶
property media¶

accounts.apps module¶

class accounts.apps.AccountsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]¶

Bases: AppConfig

App configuration for the accounts application.

name = 'accounts'¶
ready()[source]¶

Connect signal handlers when the app registry is fully populated.

accounts.forms module¶

class accounts.forms.BootstrapFormMixin(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

Mixin that adds Bootstrap’s ‘form-control’ CSS class to every visible form widget. Applied by iterating over fields in __init__ so subclasses need no per-field configuration.

class accounts.forms.RegistrationForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: BootstrapFormMixin, UserCreationForm

Unified registration form for all user roles.

A ‘role’ dropdown lets the user self-select Reader, Journalist, or Editor. The view reads this choice and sets it on the user instance before saving.

Improvements over the two-form pattern¶

  • Single form replaces separate per-role registration forms.

  • email is validated for uniqueness before saving.

  • No unused fields.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Form configuration: model, field order.

fields = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'password1', 'password2']¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
clean_email()[source]¶

Ensure the submitted email address is not already registered.

declared_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)[source]¶

Save the user with the selected role applied.

accounts.models module¶

accounts.models¶

Custom user model for the NewsApp accounts module.

Models¶

CustomUser

Extends Django’s AbstractUser with a role field and reader subscription relations.

class accounts.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Extended user model supporting three roles: Reader, Journalist, Editor.

Variables:

  • role (str) – The user’s assigned role; one of reader, journalist, or editor.

  • email (str) – Unique email address used for login and notifications.

  • bio (str) – Optional biographical text displayed on the profile page.

  • subscribed_publishers (ManyToManyField(Publisher)) – Publishers the reader follows.

  • subscribed_journalists (ManyToManyField(CustomUser)) – Journalists the reader follows.

Note

Role is set at registration and drives group assignment via a post_save signal in accounts/signals.py.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'), ('editor', 'Editor')]¶
articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

bio¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

date_joined¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

email¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)¶
groups¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_active¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_editor()[source]¶

Return True if this user has the Editor role or is a superuser.

Returns:

True if role == 'editor' or is_superuser.

Return type:

bool

is_journalist()[source]¶

Return True if this user has the Journalist role.

Returns:

True if role == 'journalist'.

Return type:

bool

is_reader()[source]¶

Return True if this user has the Reader role.

Returns:

True if role == 'reader'.

Return type:

bool

is_staff¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_superuser¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

last_login¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

last_name¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

logentry_set¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

password¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

readers¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

role¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

subscribed_journalists¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

subscribed_publishers¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

user_permissions¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

username¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

accounts.signals module¶

accounts.signals.assign_group_on_save(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]¶

Assign the user to their role group on every save.

Removes the user from all other role groups first so that a role change is immediately reflected in permissions without requiring a manual admin action.

accounts.tests module¶

Unit tests for the accounts app.

Covers user model methods, registration form validation, and auth views.

class accounts.tests.CustomUserRoleTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the CustomUser role helper methods.

test_email_is_unique()[source]¶

Creating two users with the same email raises an IntegrityError.

test_is_editor_returns_true_for_editor()[source]¶

is_editor() returns True only for users with role=’editor’.

test_is_journalist_returns_true_for_journalist()[source]¶

is_journalist() returns True for users with role=’journalist’.

test_is_reader_returns_true_for_reader()[source]¶

is_reader() returns True only for users with role=’reader’.

class accounts.tests.LoginViewTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the /accounts/login/ view.

setUp()[source]¶

Create a reader and store login URL.

test_invalid_credentials_stay_on_login()[source]¶

Wrong password keeps the user on the login page.

test_login_page_loads()[source]¶

GET /accounts/login/ returns 200.

test_logout_redirects()[source]¶

Logging out redirects to the home page.

test_valid_credentials_log_user_in()[source]¶

Correct credentials authenticate the user and redirect.

class accounts.tests.RegisterViewTests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the /accounts/register/ view.

setUp()[source]¶

Store register URL for re-use.

test_duplicate_email_rejected()[source]¶

Registering with an already-used email shows a form error.

test_register_page_loads()[source]¶

GET /accounts/register/ returns 200.

test_successful_registration_creates_user()[source]¶

Valid POST creates a new user and redirects.

accounts.tests.make_user(username='user1', role='reader', password='Pass1234!')[source]¶

Create and return a CustomUser with the given role.

accounts.urls module¶

accounts.views module¶

accounts.views¶

HTTP view functions for the NewsApp accounts module.

Views¶

register

User registration form; logs in and redirects on success.

login_user

Authentication form; redirects to role-appropriate page on success.

logout_user

Logs out the current user and redirects to the article list.

_role_redirect

Internal helper; returns a redirect based on the user’s role.

accounts.views.login_user(request)[source]¶

Authenticate and log in a user.

On success, redirect to the role-appropriate landing page. On failure, display an error message and re-render the login form. Authenticated users are redirected away immediately.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered login form on GET/failed POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

accounts.views.logout_user(request)[source]¶

Log out the current user and redirect to the article list.

Accepts both GET and POST so a simple link or a form button work.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Redirect to the article list.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

accounts.views.register(request)[source]¶

Handle user registration for all roles via a single form.

Authenticated users are redirected away immediately. On successful registration the user is logged in and redirected to their role-appropriate landing page.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – The incoming HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered registration form on GET/invalid POST, or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse
